# Harness Principles

When designing Claude Code components, these 10 principles predict whether the component will help or constrain the agent over time.

Read the single-line summary of each principle below. When a specific principle matters for your current work, open the corresponding `references/NN-*.md` for full detail, signs of violation, and examples.

## The 10 Principles

1. **Progressive Disclosure** — see `references/01-progressive-disclosure.md`
2. **See Like an Agent** — see `references/02-see-like-an-agent.md`
3. **Gotchas Over Generics** — see `references/03-gotchas-over-generics.md`
4. **Deterministic Where Possible** — see `references/04-deterministic-where-possible.md`
5. **Description Is a Trigger** — see `references/05-description-is-a-trigger.md`
6. **Avoid Railroading** — see `references/06-avoid-railroading.md`
7. **Composition Over Reconstruction** — see `references/07-composition-over-reconstruction.md`
8. **Separate Generate & Evaluate** — see `references/08-separate-generate-evaluate.md`
9. **Stress-Test Assumptions** — see `references/09-stress-test-assumptions.md`
10. **Measure What You Ship** — see `references/10-measure-what-you-ship.md`

## When to consult which reference

| Situation | Read |
|-----------|------|
| Writing a description or trigger | references/05-description-is-a-trigger.md |
| Designing a Hook or deterministic check | references/04-deterministic-where-possible.md |
| Splitting a large file or designing a Skill | references/01-progressive-disclosure.md |
| Reviewing a subagent or evaluation role | references/02-see-like-an-agent.md, references/08-separate-generate-and-evaluate.md |
| Planning v0.2 or revisiting an old decision | references/09-stress-test-assumptions.md |
| Deciding whether to build something new | references/07-composition-over-reconstruction.md |

Start here, not in references/. Load references only when the principle matters for the task at hand.
